Joveo’s Post

[](https://www.linkedin.com/company/joveo?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Joveo](https://www.linkedin.com/company/joveo?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

327,247 followers

1y

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fjoveo_the-ultimate-list-top-30-hr-influencers-activity-7283180657239875585-LdrS\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Recruitment isn’t what it used to be – and that’s a good thing. AI. Remote work. Shifting expectations. Hiring has never been more exciting or more challenging. But if you’re asking, “Who’s actually shaping the future of recruitment?” – we’ve got answers. [https://bit.ly/4fQGsrJ](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fbit%2Ely%2F4fQGsrJ\&urlhash=L0Jw\&trk=public_post-text) Meet the 30 voices leading the charge... in no particular order, but we did keep Chad 'n' Joel together! 1. [Josh Bersin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bersin?trk=public_post-text) 2. [Amy Miller](https://www.linkedin.com/in/amymil?trk=public_post-text) 3. [William Tincup](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tincup?trk=public_post-text) 4. [Meghan M. Biro](https://www.linkedin.com/in/meghanmbiro?trk=public_post-text) 5. [Tim Sackett, SCP 🟦](https://www.linkedin.com/in/timsackett?trk=public_post-text) Sackett 6. [Chad Sowash](https://pt.linkedin.com/in/chadsowash?trk=public_post-text) 7. [Joel Cheesman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joelcheesman?trk=public_post-text) 8. [Roberta Matuson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/roberta-matuson?trk=public_post-text) 9. [Ben Eubanks](https://www.linkedin.com/in/beneubanks?trk=public_post-text) 10. [Carrie Corbin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/carriecorbin?trk=public_post-text) 11. [Matt Charney](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattcharney?trk=public_post-text) 12. [Katrina Kibben](https://www.linkedin.com/in/katrinakibben?trk=public_post-text) 13. [Craig Fisher](https://www.linkedin.com/in/wcraigfisher?trk=public_post-text) 14. [Christopher Rainey](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/chrisraineyhr?trk=public_post-text) 15. [Gerry Crispin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gerrycrispin?trk=public_post-text) 16. Joel Lalgee 17. [Serge Boudreau](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/serge-boudreau-861b267?trk=public_post-text) 18. [Shelley Billinghurst](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/shelley-billinghurst-91ab71?trk=public_post-text) 19. [Sailesh Hota](https://in.linkedin.com/in/saileshhotaxlri?trk=public_post-text) 20. [Ryan Stene](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanstene?trk=public_post-text) 21. [Madeline Laurano](https://www.linkedin.com/in/madelinelaurano?trk=public_post-text) 22. [Jim Durbin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jimdurbin?trk=public_post-text) 23. [Adam Posner](https://www.linkedin.com/in/adamjposner?trk=public_post-text) 24. [Job Board Doctor](https://www.linkedin.com/company/job-board-doctor?trk=public_post-text) 25. [Hung Lee](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/hunglee?trk=public_post-text) 26. [Jeff Dickey-Chasins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dickeychasins?trk=public_post-text) 27. [Prafulla Deori](https://in.linkedin.com/in/prafulladeori?trk=public_post-text) 28. [Bill Banham](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/bill-banham-a932a0b?trk=public_post-text) 29. Sven Elbert 30. [Kris Dunn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/krisdunn?trk=public_post-text) If you made it to the end of this post, you MUST tell: who’s your favorite? If you've got a fav we've missed, give them a shoutout in the comments. [#HRInfluencers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrinfluencers\&trk=public_post-text) [#Recruitment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frecruitment\&trk=public_post-text) [#FutureofWork](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofwork\&trk=public_post-text) [#RecruitmentTrends](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frecruitmenttrends\&trk=public_post-text) [#Influencers2025](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finfluencers2025\&trk=public_post-text) [#Joveo](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjoveo\&trk=public_post-text)

[Ryan Stene](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanstene?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name) 1y

[Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fjoveo_the-ultimate-list-top-30-hr-influencers-activity-7283180657239875585-LdrS\&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

I am beyond honored and humbled to be named a Top 30 Influencer in recruitment – this list is impressive with star-studded talent. 😊 🙏 In a time when AI, candidate experience, and DEI initiatives are reshaping our industry, influencers play a critical role in driving conversations, inspiring innovation, and ensuring progress. Recruitment is evolving faster than ever, and it’s essential that we continue to lead with empathy, inclusivity, and a commitment to creating meaningful connections. Thank you to those who inspire me daily—together, we’re shaping the future of work! What’s is one of your strategic initiatives this year as your drive transformation?

[Papiya Soni](https://in.linkedin.com/in/papiya-soni-a54958111?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo Edited

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fpapiya-soni-a54958111_socialmediastrategy-digitalrecruitment-activity-7381952428419710977-HaG-\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

🚀 𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐌𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐚 𝐈𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐉𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐁𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 — 𝐈𝐭’𝐬 𝐚 𝐏𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐮𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐫𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐓𝐨𝐨𝐥 💼 In today’s competitive talent market, traditional job boards alone are not enough to attract top talent. As HR professionals, we need to meet candidates where they are — and that’s on 𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗺𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮. Here are a few 𝗲𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗺𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗿𝘂𝗶𝘁𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗲𝘀 that are transforming hiring: 1️⃣ 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱 𝗮 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗴 𝗘𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗲𝗿 𝗕𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗱 Your company’s social presence reflects its culture. Share real employee stories, behind-the-scenes moments, and achievements. Authenticity attracts the right talent. 2️⃣ 𝗟𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗠𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗽𝗹𝗲 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝘀 While LinkedIn remains a top choice for professional hiring, don’t underestimate the power of Instagram, Facebook, and X (formerly Twitter) for showcasing culture and reaching passive candidates. 3️⃣ 𝗘𝗻𝗴𝗮𝗴𝗲, 𝗗𝗼𝗻’𝘁 𝗝𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁 Recruitment isn’t a one-way street. Respond to comments, interact in niche groups, and participate in discussions. Engagement builds trust. 4️⃣ 𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗧𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗔𝗱𝘀 & 𝗛𝗮𝘀𝗵𝘁𝗮𝗴𝘀 Smart targeting helps reach specific skill sets and locations. Branded hashtags can create a consistent recruitment campaign identity. 5️⃣ 𝗘𝗺𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿 𝗘𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗲𝗲𝘀 𝗮𝘀 𝗔𝗺𝗯𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗮𝗱𝗼𝗿𝘀 Employee advocacy goes a long way. Encourage your team to share openings and company updates. A trusted voice speaks louder than a job ad. 📊 A strong social media recruitment strategy doesn’t just fill positions — it builds long-term connections with the talent market. Let’s not just hire for today. Let’s build communities for tomorrow. 💬✨ [#SocialMediaStrategy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsocialmediastrategy\&trk=public_post-text) [#DigitalRecruitment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdigitalrecruitment\&trk=public_post-text) [#HiringBestPractices](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhiringbestpractices\&trk=public_post-text) [#HRInsights](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrinsights\&trk=public_post-text) [#TalentEngagement](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftalentengagement\&trk=public_post-text) [#HRCommunity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrcommunity\&trk=public_post-text) [#RecruitmentStrategy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frecruitmentstrategy\&trk=public_post-text)

[3](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fpapiya-soni-a54958111_socialmediastrategy-digitalrecruitment-activity-7381952428419710977-HaG-\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/eric-buntin_employerbranding-recruitmentmarketing-activity-7383880832953847808-K64M)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eric-buntin?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Eric Buntin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eric-buntin?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feric-buntin_employerbranding-recruitmentmarketing-activity-7383880832953847808-K64M\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

🚨 TA Leaders: It’s time to meet talent where they are. 🚨 If your employer brand isn’t showing up on TikTok, Instagram, or Snapchat… you might be missing out on your next great hire. 📱👀 Today’s candidates—especially early career—aren’t just reading job descriptions. They’re watching “day in the life” videos, scrolling memes, and looking for authentic employee voices. 🎥💬 ✅ 73% of grads say they’re more likely to apply after seeing employer content online. ✅ Candidates who engage with brand content move faster, respond more, and accept more offers. ✅ Real stories from real employees > polished CEO videos. The new playbook? 🎯 Modular, authentic, employee-driven content 🎯 Multi-channel strategy (yes, even Spotify and [Weather.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2FWeather%2Ecom\&urlhash=f7y-\&trk=public_post-text)) 🎯 Tracking the entire candidate journey—not just clicks Let’s stop thinking of employer branding as a “nice to have.” It’s a strategic lever for recruitment ROI. 💡📈 💬 Curious how your team is activating employee voices across social? Drop a comment or DM—always up for sharing ideas. TalentAcquisition [#EmployerBranding](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Femployerbranding\&trk=public_post-text) [#RecruitmentMarketing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frecruitmentmarketing\&trk=public_post-text) [#GenZHiring](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fgenzhiring\&trk=public_post-text) [#HRStrategy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrstrategy\&trk=public_post-text) [#SocialRecruiting](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsocialrecruiting\&trk=public_post-text) [#EmployeeExperience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Femployeeexperience\&trk=public_post-text) [#FutureOfWork](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofwork\&trk=public_post-text) Would you like help tailoring this further for a specific audience or campaign?

[17](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Feric-buntin_employerbranding-recruitmentmarketing-activity-7383880832953847808-K64M\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[Sunita Fernandes](https://in.linkedin.com/in/sunita-fernandes-75a78182?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsunita-fernandes-75a78182_talentacquisition-employerbranding-futureofwork-activity-7382121925860421632-kXTp\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗺𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗶𝘀𝗻’𝘁 𝗷𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗮 𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗱 — 𝗶𝘁’𝘀 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗵𝗶𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆 Not long ago, job boards and career pages were the main bridges between talent and employers. Today, that bridge has expanded and it’s built on 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗸𝗲𝗱𝗜𝗻, 𝗜𝗻𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗺, 𝗫 (𝗧𝘄𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿), and even 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗧𝘂𝗯𝗲. ✨ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗳𝘁: Candidates are no longer just job seekers. They are active content consumers, creators, and community members. Your employer brand is being judged not just by your job ad, but by your digital presence, your culture stories, and your leadership voices online. 𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝗶𝗻 𝗛𝗶𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴: • Reach where talent lives → Social platforms are the first touchpoint for both active and passive candidates. • Storytelling builds trust → Posts about culture, DEI, growth, and impact create emotional connections. • Human faces matter → Leaders, employees, and alumni become powerful brand ambassadors. • Data-driven insights → AI tools can analyze engagement, candidate pools, and brand sentiment in real time. 𝗣𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗪𝗮𝘆𝘀 𝗧𝗼 𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗶𝗻 𝗛𝗶𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴: 1. Employer Branding Campaigns – Showcase real employee stories, day-in-life posts, and behind-the-scenes moments. 2. Leadership Visibility – Encourage leaders to share authentic insights, not just press releases. 3. Community Engagement – Respond to comments, join talent groups, and host live Q\&As. 4. Targeted Talent Ads – Use AI-backed social campaigns to reach niche skill sets. 5. Content + Careers – Blend thought leadership with opportunities, candidates don’t just want jobs, they want purpose. 💡 Example: A pharmaceutical company launching a new R\&D wing could run a social campaign highlighting their scientists, sharing innovation stories, and tying it to open roles. The message? We’re not just hiring for jobs, we’re inviting you to shape the future of healthcare. 𝗕𝗼𝘁𝘁𝗼𝗺 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗲 Social media isn’t a trend to follow. It’s the stage where your employer brand performs daily. And the companies that win talent tomorrow are the ones investing in digital storytelling today. 💭 How is your organization using social media in hiring; transactionally, or strategically? [#TalentAcquisition](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftalentacquisition\&trk=public_post-text) [#EmployerBranding](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Femployerbranding\&trk=public_post-text) [#FutureOfWork](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofwork\&trk=public_post-text) [#SocialHiring](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsocialhiring\&trk=public_post-text) [#AIinHR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faiinhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#CultureMatters](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fculturematters\&trk=public_post-text) [#HRInsights](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrinsights\&trk=public_post-text) [#SunitaFernandesInsights](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsunitafernandesinsights\&trk=public_post-text)

To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsunita-fernandes-75a78182_talentacquisition-employerbranding-futureofwork-activity-7382121925860421632-kXTp\&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/carlosmdelpozo_i-see-a-lot-of-advice-on-social-media-about-activity-7378855841359306752-2cmc)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/carlosmdelpozo?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Carlos del Pozo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/carlosmdelpozo?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcarlosmdelpozo_i-see-a-lot-of-advice-on-social-media-about-activity-7378855841359306752-2cmc\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I see a lot of advice on social media about hiring. Some of it’s helpful, but too much of it comes without key context. Take “hire slow” as an example. That works for strong brands that candidates are lining up for. You can take your time because the best people will tolerate the wait. But if you’re in a crowded market where talent is scarce and competitors are moving fast, “hire slow” can actually work against you, because the best candidates just know that you’re… slow. Which is to say: 𝗘𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆 𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗱𝗶𝗳𝗳𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘁. 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗻𝘆, 𝗶𝗻 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗶𝗻𝗱𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆, 𝗮𝘁 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝗴𝗿𝗼𝘄𝘁𝗵, 𝗺𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗹𝘆 𝗯𝗮𝗰𝗸𝗳𝗶𝗿𝗲 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿. That’s why you can’t build your hiring strategy around copy-pasted tactics or shortcuts. You need a strategy that’s grounded in your market, your employer brand, and your level of competition. From there, the right tactics will emerge. There’s no silver bullet in hiring. The companies that win are the ones that know their market, play to their strengths, and build a robust, repeatable process that fits them… 𝘯𝘰𝘵 𝘴𝘰𝘮𝘦𝘰𝘯𝘦 𝘦𝘭𝘴𝘦. At Team Engine, we give you the tools and guidance to build it!

[18](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcarlosmdelpozo_i-see-a-lot-of-advice-on-social-media-about-activity-7378855841359306752-2cmc\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [4 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcarlosmdelpozo_i-see-a-lot-of-advice-on-social-media-about-activity-7378855841359306752-2cmc\&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/chris-adah_linkedin-branding-employment-activity-7385234862099746816-R_Ev)

[](https://ng.linkedin.com/in/chris-adah?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Chris aDah](https://ng.linkedin.com/in/chris-adah?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

7mo Edited

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fchris-adah_linkedin-branding-employment-activity-7385234862099746816-R_Ev\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Let us Talk About Hiring Social Media Managers you would not let Work Everyone is talking about toxic bosses and bad work environments, but here is one red flag no one is calling out enough: " You hire a Social Media Manager… and then micromanage every step of their job" You need approvals for captions, designs, even hashtags, from people who do not actually understand the trends, platform or strategy. Like in this era of business automations, they still need you to work manually so it suits their micromanagement. While you are busy second-guessing their expertise, you're demanding 6-hour design work in 20 minutes and ignoring strategy decks and content calendars they carefully prepared. Here's the thing, If you need a professional, it’s because you don’t know how to do the job. That’s OK, that’s why you hired them and if you are not going to trust them to do it, why hire them in the first place? Social media isn’t guesswork. It’s not vibes and luck. It's process. Strategy. Timing. Algorithms. Testing. Insight. Execution. You do not post when you feel like it, you post when your audience is online. You do not design in 5 minutes, you craft content that works for your brand, market, and goals. The wildest part? Many of these same people are sitting in top positions. It is not just frustrating, it is bad business. - If you want growth, let your experts be experts. - If you want engagement, trust the process. - If you want results, stop being the bottleneck. Social media management is not just about posting and captioning, you are a dashboard analyst, you monitor, track and schedule using 3rd party apps made available by those who understand trends and smart works. In my opinion, the only solution to this menace is hiring external bodies, in partnership with the Ministry of Labor and Employment, to vet employees. These " bribeless" bodies will not be biased and will curb unnecessary sacks from these companies. A lot of people have lost their jobs when they were actually doing what they were hired to do. This cohort need protection. I believe we have over emphasized on this matter. Now is the time to take actions. Micromanagement is not leadership. It is insecurity with a title. Hire people and let them do their jobs. [#linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flinkedin\&trk=public_post-text) [#branding](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbranding\&trk=public_post-text) [#employment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Femployment\&trk=public_post-text) [#Toxicbosses](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftoxicbosses\&trk=public_post-text)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fnataliaverna_social-media-hiring-trends-pierpoint-activity-7378817583606620160-c0o3\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I’ve noticed how social media has intersected with hiring lately… and wow, it’s changed rapidly. We’ve moved from static job boards to scrollable employer branding. [Pierpoint International](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pierpoint?trk=public_post-text) International published a useful article breaking down how hiring trends are shifting and how you can stay ahead of the curve. My takeaways are: 💬 Every job post is a chance to tell a story. 💡 Every comment or DM is an invitation to connect. If you’re relying on the same strategy from 5 years ago, this article’s a must-read: [https://bit.ly/45QFpF9](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fbit%2Ely%2F45QFpF9\&urlhash=fD8K\&trk=public_post-text) [#SocialHiring](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsocialhiring\&trk=public_post-text) [#RecruitmentTrends](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frecruitmenttrends\&trk=public_post-text) [#FutureOfWork](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofwork\&trk=public_post-text) [#EmployerBrand](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Femployerbrand\&trk=public_post-text) [#TalentAttraction](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftalentattraction\&trk=public_post-text)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stuart-logan-5138a32?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Stuart Logan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stuart-logan-5138a32?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fstuart-logan-5138a32_stop-crowning-hr-influencers-who-haven-activity-7380602208150593536-7XNo\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Stop Crowning HR “Influencers” Who Haven’t Earned It. In HR, influence matters. The voices we let shape our thinking end up shaping our teams, our strategies, and ultimately our businesses. Which is why it bothers me when I see “influencers” with a thin track record suddenly positioned as experts. I just saw a opinionated “business” leader push why merging IT and HR was something other business groups have done long ago and HR are just laggards, looking at his profile and 20 years out of the workforce working for “their surname” consulting company. Contrast that with someone like [Josh Bersin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bersin?trk=public_post-text). Love him or not, he’s earned it. He does the research, he connects with the vendors, he balances perspectives without alienating stakeholders. He brings credibility because he’s been consistent, professional, and evidence-based. Others have got real experience from analytics, to head of talent Mgt in Large Pharma, to consultancies specializing in employee experience. But scroll LinkedIn and you’ll see another pattern: • 1 year as an “independent consultant” • 2 years at a company, then a jump • Another 18 months in a role that doesn’t line up with the story they’re selling Maybe they’re a genius. But my money says they haven’t been through the real cycle of transformation, making the big bets, fixing the mistakes, carrying the scars. They project competence without the lived experience. In all the companies I’ve had the honor to support transformation the path is never the same because the starting point is not the same, the incumbent platforms and processes are not the same and the people and politics are not the same, so you can not tackle the transformations the same. And here’s the problem: HR already fights enough battles with credibility. If we let theorists masquerading as practitioners set the agenda, we dilute the function. The best HR ideas don’t just come from models and frameworks, they come from doing the work. From leading change when it’s messy, when it’s political, when it’s imperfect. So, my take: we need to be more discerning about who we let influence our profession. • Follow the researchers who bring data. • Listen to the practitioners who’ve carried the weight of decisions. • Be skeptical of shiny theory without evidence of practice. HR is too important to be led by people who’ve only told the story l, and never lived it. What’s your thoughts on this topic?


8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamradhi-tripathi-4669aa1b3_sociallistening-talentacquisition-hr-activity-7378689915045404672-n6PF\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

🌟 Part 2: How Social Listening Works (Breaking It Down) Welcome back to my Social Listening series! 👋 In my last post, we explored what Social Listening really is. Today, let’s talk about how it actually works—and why it matters for us in Talent Acquisition. Think of Social Listening as a simple 4-step process: 1️⃣ Monitoring – Keeping an eye on platforms like LinkedIn, Glassdoor, Reddit, Twitter (X), or niche forums where candidates share their experiences and thoughts. 2️⃣ Gathering Data – Every comment, review, or post adds up. Together, they reveal patterns and insights that we might otherwise miss. 3️⃣ Understanding Sentiment – Are candidates excited, neutral, or frustrated? Capturing this emotional pulse is pure gold—it tells us what’s working and what needs attention. 4️⃣ Acting on Insights – The most critical step. If candidates say interview feedback takes too long, we can fix it. If they love flexibility, we can highlight it in our job ads. It’s not just about collecting data—it’s about listening with the intent to improve, to make experiences better for both candidates and the organization. 👉 In Part 3, I’ll share why Social Listening is a true game-changer in recruitment and how it helps us stand out as an employer of choice. Until then, I’d love to hear from you: have you ever changed a hiring process or strategy based on what you read online? [#SocialListening](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsociallistening\&trk=public_post-text) [#TalentAcquisition](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftalentacquisition\&trk=public_post-text) [#HR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#CandidateExperience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcandidateexperience\&trk=public_post-text)

[11](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamradhi-tripathi-4669aa1b3_sociallistening-talentacquisition-hr-activity-7378689915045404672-n6PF\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamradhi-tripathi-4669aa1b3_sociallistening-talentacquisition-hr-activity-7378689915045404672-n6PF\&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsamradhi-tripathi-4669aa1b3_sociallistening-talentacquisition-hr-activity-7378689915045404672-n6PF\&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

[Martins Rafiu](https://ng.linkedin.com/in/martins-rafiu-202865272?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fmartins-rafiu-202865272_socialmedia-digitalmarketing-careergrowth-activity-7381609513348628480-jB5I\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

What I’ve Learned in My First Week Exploring Social Media Management 🌐 Transitioning from HR into social media management has been an exciting shift — and this first week reminded me that both fields have one thing in common: people. Here are a few things I’ve learned so far 😊 🔵 Content strategy is people strategy. Just like in HR, understanding your audience — their needs, pain points, and motivations — is what drives engagement. 🟡 Consistency builds trust. Whether managing employees or online communities, showing up regularly and authentically makes all the difference. 🟢 Data drives better communication. In HR, we measure performance; in social media, we measure engagement — both help refine our approach for better impact. I’m enjoying how this new path connects creativity with communication and community building — skills that have always been part of my HR foundation. If you’ve ever transitioned careers or expanded your professional interests, what helped you the most during that shift? 💭 [#SocialMedia](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsocialmedia\&trk=public_post-text) [#DigitalMarketing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdigitalmarketing\&trk=public_post-text) [#CareerGrowth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcareergrowth\&trk=public_post-text) [#ContentCreation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcontentcreation\&trk=public_post-text) [#HR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#LearningJourney](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flearningjourney\&trk=public_post-text)

[1](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fmartins-rafiu-202865272_socialmedia-digitalmarketing-careergrowth-activity-7381609513348628480-jB5I\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [1 Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fmartins-rafiu-202865272_socialmedia-digitalmarketing-careergrowth-activity-7381609513348628480-jB5I\&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)

LinkedIn

X

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/elzettefourie_social-media-changes-recruitment-recruiting-activity-7378696462823653376-3js5)

[](https://za.linkedin.com/in/elzettefourie?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Elzette Fourie](https://za.linkedin.com/in/elzettefourie?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

8mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Felzettefourie_social-media-changes-recruitment-recruiting-activity-7378696462823653376-3js5\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Social media is transforming recruitment, with platforms like LinkedIn, Instagram, and Twitter (X) helping companies find top talent more efficiently. Global brands like Microsoft use LinkedIn for targeted hiring, while Unilever shares workplace stories to attract culturally aligned candidates. Even small startups are benefiting, with one reporting over half of its hires coming from Instagram. Social media offers faster recruitment, cost savings, access to diverse talent, and insights into candidates' personalities, making it a powerful tool for modern hiring strategies.

\[

Social Media Changes Recruitment | Recruiting News Network recruitingnewsnetwork.com

]\(https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Erecruitingnewsnetwork%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fbeyond-the-resume-how-social-media-is-changing-talent-acquisition\&urlhash=\_zPc\&trk=public\_post\_feed-article-content)
